text:
Andrew Cotter made a pun that hadTwitterusers chuckling as theOlympic Games in Pariswere referred to as "Seine-sational" in reference to the city's famous river.

BBCviewers heard: "The dare I say it, Seine-sational games", before Andrew paused to deliver an "ugh" which some interpreted as the ultimate disdainful swipe. "Nothing has ever been louder in this universe than Andrew Cotter's "Ugh." after that "Seine-sational" pun. #ClosingCeremony," tweeted one viewer.

She added: "Also, we've finally captured the sound of 75000 people recoiling in unison." Another chimed in via the comments: "I absolutely howled with laughter! Never had more disdain been expressed in so short an utterance!"

"We were a moment away from a 'please clap'," quipped a third. Continuing the pun, another branded him "inseinely brave" for cracking the joke.

It comes after multiple athletes fell ill following their Olympic swims in the famous Seine, leading to fears that they may have been exposed to E.coli bacteria in the water. On Sunday, it emerged that three athletes from the German team were treated as hospital outpatientsafter suffering nausea, vomitting and diarrhea.

READ MORE:Hazel Irvine's remarks cause Olympics frenzy as fans make Andrew Cotter demand

nothing has ever been louder in this universe than Andrew Cotter's "Ugh." after that "Seine-sational" pun.#ClosingCeremony??????pic.twitter.com/cR4NhpQgCU

Meanwhile, earlier in the montha Belgian contestant was forced to pull out of the mixed relay after also coming down with a suspected E.coli infection and being hospitalised.

A Norwegian triathlete was also left ill, citing a stomach virus, but stated it was "hard to say" whether or not exposure to the river was responsible.

Others had also fallen ill, following a series of tests that showed bacterial levels were sometimes higher than World Aquatics standards deem acceptable.

However, Paris Mayor Anne Hidalgo staged a swim in the famous river herself last month in a bid to prove it was safe.

Don't miss:Gabby Logan issues brutal Olympics closing ceremony verdict with one word dig

Don't miss:Tom Cruise causes concern as 'upset' Olympics viewers issue stern warning



She stated: "We will of course wait to get the results of the water quality but the event will take place because there’s been a clear improvement of the weather these past few days.

"I’m really proud and happy and to all those who want to continue saying it’s impossible to depollute a river, I tell them, ’Yes it’s possible, we did it.'"

It had previously been made illegal to swim in the Seine after a ruling in 1923 about water toxicity, but Paris invested €1.4bn in infrastructure improvements to make it more plausible for events to take place in the water.

However, water quality can be dependent on the weather, with the sun's UV rays having antibacterial properties, while heavy rains can cause more polluted wastewater to flow into the river, raising the bacterial levels.

title: Andrew Cotter lets slip brutal Olympics swipe in blink-and-you-miss-it moment | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Chloe Govan
description: Broadcaster Andrew Cotter has been accused of expressing 'disdain' towards the Paris Olympics during his closing ceremony speech.
